purpose: Per-variant production counts (units produced) at body+drivetrain level for every Porsche sports car generation and variant in lib/era-content/generation-content.ts that is NOT already covered by tonight's 996.2 Carrera hand-curation session.

exclude_generation_key_notes.992.2: Production ongoing (MY2025–present). Full-generation and per-variant totals unavailable.

scope.exclude_specific_variants_notes: These 7 996.2 Carrera variants are being hand-curated in a parallel overnight session and should NOT be duplicated here. 996.2 Turbo, Turbo S, GT2 Mk1, GT2 Mk2, GT3 Mk2 ARE in scope.

research_goal: For each variant in scope, record: units_produced (worldwide total), regional_splits where available (US/North America, Europe, ROW), body_style_splits where available (Coupe vs Cabriolet vs Targa for variants produced in multiple bodies), sources consulted, date_checked, and a confidence_tag.

sources_required.notes: Primary source for modern water-cooled 911 variant breakdowns; provided the 996.2 Carrera data
Cross-check for water-cooled era aggregate figures
Generation pages often include production tables; good for GT variant caps (GT3 RS 4.0 = 600, GT2 RS = 500, etc.)
German specialist; Porsche model history pages include production figures
Model profile pages include production data for GT and limited variants
Forums; use for disputed or low-volume variant figures where community consensus exists
Official production announcement data; best for modern generations (992, 991 halo variants)
Excellence Magazine archives; strong for air-cooled variants and historical data
UK 911 specialist; production data for limited variants

confidence_tags.high: Published by Porsche AG, Porsche Newsroom, or official press release; OR two independent reputable sources agree within 5%
confidence_tags.medium: Single reputable source (Supercar Nostalgia, Wikipedia with cited reference, jtcars, Elferspot, Excellence Magazine)
confidence_tags.low: Derived figure, rough estimate, or sources in conflict with no clear resolution; note the conflict explicitly

methodology.conflict_handling: If sources conflict by >5%, record ALL source figures, flag as conflict:true, note the discrepancy, and assign confidence:low. Do not pick one without documented reasoning.
methodology.already_verified_variants: Many variants in generation-content.ts already have production fields populated. These should be verified against sources but NOT overwritten unless a clear error is found — flag discrepancies instead.
methodology.regional_splits_priority: For 911 variants (not 356 or Boxster/Cayman), US/North America splits are high-value. Record worldwide total first, then regional if available.
methodology.limited_edition_precision: For production-capped limited editions, record exact figures when officially announced (e.g., 911 R = 991 exactly, GT3 RS 4.0 = 600 exactly). Use ~ prefix only for non-capped variants where the figure is an estimate.

variants.existing_production_field: string or null (copy existing value from generation-content.ts if present)

output_format.important: DO NOT auto-commit to generation-content.ts. Output ONLY to this staging file. User review required before any edits to generation-content.ts.

variants_with_figures_already_in_content.note: These are populated as of the spec creation date. The batch should verify them, not blindly skip them.

variants_missing_figures_priority_target.note: These are variants where the generation-content.ts production field is null/absent and the figure would have high collector-market utility. Prioritise these in the batch.
boxster_cayman: all variants — 986, 987.1/2 Boxster, 981, 982/718 Boxster, 987.1/2 Cayman, 981 Cayman, 982/718 Cayman

known_risks.detail: Excellence Magazine archives may require subscription access. Total 911 PDFs are not publicly indexed. Fallback: Rennlist thread citations where original Excellence sources are quoted.
Fetching 100+ individual source pages in a single session may trigger rate limiting on some aggregator sites. Space lookups with appropriate delays.
Production figure for 991.2 GT2 RS is already flagged as conflicting (~1,000–1,580 worldwide). Multiple sources disagree. This conflict may persist after research; document all cited values.
For 356 and early 911 variants, per-body-style production splits may not be independently verifiable across two sources. Accept single reputable source (confidence:medium) for these generations.

Porsche does not release per-variant production figures for Boxster/Cayman to the same standard as the 911. Data from Supercar Nostalgia or jtcars may be the best available; confidence will often be medium.
If using the Claude API batch script execution mechanism: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is not in .env.local or .env.example. Key must be added before the script can run.
